taskDescription: Click on the key biochemistry terms related to the extracellular matrix in the text below.
textField:
*Collagen* is the most abundant *protein* in the human body, forming a characteristic *triple* *helix* from three polypeptide *alpha* chains. Every third residue must be *glycine*, and the X-Y positions are typically occupied by *proline* and *hydroxyproline*. The *hydroxylation* reactions require *ascorbic* acid as a cofactor, and deficiency leads to *scurvy*.

*Type* *I* collagen is found in bone, skin, and tendon. Mutations cause *osteogenesis* *imperfecta*. *Type* *III* collagen is found in blood vessel walls, and its defects cause *Ehlers-Danlos* syndrome. *Type* *IV* collagen forms the *basement* *membrane* as a sheet-like network.

Other matrix components include *elastin* for tissue recoil, *fibronectin* for cell-matrix adhesion via *integrins*, *proteoglycans* containing *glycosaminoglycans* for hydration, and *laminin* in the basement membrane.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review extracellular matrix concepts and try again.
Good effort! Review the areas you missed.
Excellent understanding of the extracellular matrix!
